messages.content:
summarize the following:

President Xi Jinping stressed the importance of building a new system for a higher-standard open economy at a key meeting on Tuesday.

Xi, who is also general secretary of the Communist Party of China Central Committee and chairman of the Central Military Commission, made the remarks while presiding over a meeting of the Central Commission for Comprehensively Deepening Reform under the 20th CPC Central Committee.

Xi, who is head of the commission, called for greater institutional opening-up in key areas of international exchange and cooperation, such as investment, trade, finance and innovation.

He also said that efforts must be made to further reform systems and mechanisms and refine relevant policies and measures in order to bring China's level of opening-up to new heights.

The meeting said that it is necessary to perfect top-level design of the new system, expand market access and improve the business environment in the country.

China must fully leverage its comprehensive advantages, attract global resources by promoting domestic circulation and enhance the quality and level of its trade and investment cooperation, the meeting's participants said.

They also called for the building of a new system of an open economy at a higher level to be closely integrated with the Belt and Road Initiative and other national strategies.

The meeting also adopted a policy document to promote the gradual transition from dual control over the amount and intensity of energy consumption to dual control over the amount and intensity of carbon emissions.

It was noted at the meeting that persistent efforts must be made to create the conditions for this transition and to strike a balance between development and carbon emission reduction according to the actual situation.

Since the 18th National Congress of the CPC in 2012, China has prioritized the pursuit of green and low-carbon development, leading to a sharp increase in the country's energy use efficiency and the continuous reduction of carbon dioxide emissions.

The country has set a target of hitting peak emissions before 2030 and achieving carbon neutrality before 2060.

Policymakers at the meeting also stressed the need to reform the salary system in colleges, universities and research institutes in an effort to encourage innovation.

Salary distribution must be closely related to performance and tilt toward those who are deeply engaged in teaching and scientific research and those making outstanding contributions to difficult and crucial tasks, they said.

They also said that those who are engaged in teaching fundamental disciplines and conducting cutting-edge research, and those making breakthroughs in core technologies in key fields should be better supported in terms of the salary system.

The meeting's participants underscored the importance of strengthening supervision of salary management to ensure that country's funds are used where they are most needed.

The meeting's participants called for efforts to further reform systems related to the gas and oil market, step up market supervision, regulate market order and promote fair competition.

It is important to deepen the reform of the oil and gas reserve system and make good use of the emergency and regulatory capabilities of the reserves, they said.

Li Qiang, Wang Huning and Cai Qi, who are members of the Standing Committee of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and deputy heads of the commission, attended the meeting.

President Xi Jinping chaired a meeting of the Central Commission for Comprehensively Deepening Reform under the CPC Central Committee where he stressed the importance of building a new system for a higher-standard open economy. He called for greater institutional opening-up in areas such as investment, trade, finance, and innovation. The meeting emphasized the need to perfect the top-level design of the new system, expand market access, improve the business environment, and attract global resources. They also highlighted the integration of the new open economy system with the Belt and Road Initiative and other national strategies. Additionally, a policy document was adopted to promote the transition from dual control over energy consumption to dual control over carbon emissions. The meeting also addressed the reform of the salary system in educational and research institutions to encourage innovation and support those engaged in teaching and research. Lastly, the participants called for further reform of the gas and oil market systems, including deepening the oil and gas reserve system and improving market supervision and fair competition.

modify "China’s attempts to encourage foreign investors have ramped up in recent weeks as it’s become clear that the economy’s recovery following the end of Beijing’s Covid Zero policies is starting to flag."
China's efforts to attract foreign investors have intensified in the past few weeks amid signs of a slowdown in the economy's recovery following the relaxation of Beijing's Covid Zero policies.

give a title to "Chinese President Xi Jinping chaired a meeting of the Central Commission for Comprehensively Deepening Reform where he stressed the importance of building a new system for a higher-standard open economy. He called for greater institutional opening-up in areas such as investment, trade, finance, and innovation to improve the business environment and attract global resources. China’s efforts to attract foreign investors have accelerated in the past few weeks amid signs of a slowdown in the economy’s recovery following the relaxation of Beijing’s Covid Zero policies."
"President Xi Jinping Calls for New and Open Economy: Accelerating Efforts to Attract Foreign Investors"
give another title
"China's Push for a Higher-Standard Open Economy: President Xi Jinping's Emphasis on Institutional Opening-up and Foreign Investment Attraction"
shorten “China’s Push for a Higher-Standard Open Economy: President Xi Jinping’s Emphasis on Institutional Opening-up and Foreign Investment Attraction”
"China's Drive for a Higher-Standard Open Economy: President Xi Jinping's Focus on Opening-up and Foreign Investment"

summarize the following:

澳門賭收正逐步復甦，金沙中國（1928）總裁王英偉於亞洲國際娛樂展（G2E）上表示，過去市場質疑澳門政府全年1300億澳門元的賭收預測能否達標，而今年上半年當地博彩毛收入已達逾801億元，故相信年内可達標並有望勝預期。

王英偉稱，公司已承諾於未來10年，將會投資逾3020億澳門元，當中的非博彩投資額達278億澳門元，目前正在推進相關項目，包括增加1.8萬平方米的金光會議展覽區域，以及位於倫敦人內的5萬平方米溫室花園的項目。

王英偉指出，澳門當地酒店房間的供應量目前達到逾4萬，但相比起其他地方仍較少，如美國拉斯維加斯酒店房間的供應量達到15萬，而新加坡則達6萬，澳門有必要提升競爭力，而當地的土地限制亦為原因之一，但相信鄰近的橫琴可補充澳門的不足。

The gambling revenue in Macau is gradually recovering, with the President of Sands China (1928), Wang Yingwei, stating at the Global Gaming Expo Asia (G2E) that the local gross gaming revenue in the first half of this year has already exceeded MOP 80.1 billion. Wang expressed confidence that it will meet the government's annual forecast of MOP 130 billion and may even surpass expectations.

Wang further announced the company's commitment to invest over MOP 302 billion in the next ten years, with MOP 27.8 billion dedicated to non-gaming investments. Ongoing projects include the expansion of the Venetian Macao's convention and exhibition space by adding 18,000 square meters, as well as the construction of a 50,000 square meter greenhouse garden within the Londoner Macao.

Wang highlighted that while Macau currently has over 40,000 hotel rooms, it still lags behind other destinations such as Las Vegas with 150,000 rooms and Singapore with 60,000 rooms. He emphasized the need for Macau to enhance its competitiveness, citing land restrictions as one of the reasons for limited room supply. However, he expressed belief that nearby Hengqin Island could help supplement Macau's deficiency.

summarize and modify "The gambling revenue in Macau is gradually recovering, with the President of Sands China (1928HK), Wang Yingwei, stating that the local gross gaming revenue in the 1H 2023 has already exceeded MOP 80.1 billion. Wang expressed confidence that it will meet the government’s annual forecast of MOP 130 billion and may even surpass expectations. Wang further announced the company’s commitment to invest over MOP 302 billion in the next ten years, with MOP 27.8 billion dedicated to non-gaming investments. "

Macau's gambling revenue is showing a gradual recovery, as per the President of Sands China (1928HK), Wang Yingwei. He revealed that in the first half of 2023, the local gross gaming revenue has surpassed MOP 80.1 billion, giving him confidence that it will meet the government's annual forecast of MOP 130 billion and potentially exceed expectations. Wang also announced Sands China's commitment to invest over MOP 302 billion in the next decade, with MOP 27.8 billion allocated for non-gaming ventures.

give a title to "Macau’s gambling revenue is showing a gradual recovery, with the President of Sands China (1928HK) Wang Yingwei stating that the local gross gaming revenue in the first half of 2023 has already exceeded MOP 80.1 billion. Wang expressed confidence that it will meet the government’s annual forecast of MOP 130 billion and potentially exceed expectations. Wang also announced the company’s commitment to invest over MOP 302 billion in the next decade, with MOP 27.8 billion dedicated to non-gaming investments. "
"Macau's Gambling Revenue Bounces Back: Sands China President Expects Exceeding Forecasts with Massive Investments"

summarize the following:

The chances of Microsoft taking over major games publisher Activision Blizzard have been given a big boost after a US judge rejected a request from US regulators to block the deal.

Microsoft said after the US win it would focus on resolving concerns in the UK.

The tech giant's merger with the Call of Duty owner would be the biggest deal of its kind in gaming industry history.

Shares in Activision surged more than 10% as investors bet it would succeed.

In the US regulators had argued that such a deal, valued at $69bn (£56bn) last year, would hurt gamers and reduce competition by giving Microsoft, maker of the Xbox, the power to deny rivals access to Activision's games.

The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) had sought an emergency block of the deal, which is due to close later this month, while it challenged the plans.

But Judge Jacqueline Scott Corley said she did not think the regulator would win in its case.

"The FTC has not shown it is likely to succeed on its assertion the combined firm will probably pull Call of Duty from Sony PlayStation, or that its ownership of Activision content will substantially lessen competition in the video game library subscription and cloud gaming markets," Judge Scott Corley wrote in her decision, delivered after a week-long hearing in San Francisco.

The ruling in the US is the strongest indicator so far that the tech giant's purchase will eventually go forward.

It comes after the deal was approved by the European Union, while a bid to block the merger in the UK is currently under appeal.

Microsoft's $69bn Activision deal approved by EU
Microsoft's Activision takeover blocked in UK
Microsoft president Brad Smith said the company was "grateful" for the quick decision and would now turn its focus to the UK.

He and the UK's Competition and Markets Authority said the two sides had agreed to put litigation on hold while the company figured out a way to address the concerns, which had focused on the cloud gaming market.

"We stand ready to consider any proposals from Microsoft to restructure the transaction in a way that would address the concerns set out in our final report," a spokesperson for the CMA said.

The developments appear poised to deliver a major win for Microsoft, which is trying to keep up with market leaders PlayStation and Nintendo by investing heavily in gaming content that might encourage players to choose its platforms, including the Xbox console, over its rivals.

Activision Blizzard is responsible for major titles including Call of Duty, World of Warcraft, Diablo and Overwatch, and also owns King, the mobile games developer responsible for Candy Crush.

The fate of the Call of Duty franchise was key to regulators' arguments.

Arguing on the side of regulators, PlayStation boss Jim Ryan had said in a video deposition that Microsoft would be likely to restrict access to the series for PlayStation users, or offer them a degraded version.

However, Microsoft said it had offered a 10-year licensing agreement to Sony for the game and argued that it would make no financial sense to restrict access to such a massive following.

"Our merger will benefit consumers and workers. It will enable competition rather than allow entrenched market leaders to continue to dominate our rapidly growing industry," Bobby Kotick, chief executive of Activision Blizzard, said after the ruling.

In a message to staff shared by the company he added: "We're optimistic that today's ruling signals a path to full regulatory approval elsewhere around the globe, and we stand ready to work with UK regulators to address any remaining concerns so our merger can quickly close."

The decision in the US is not necessarily the end of the process. The FTC can appeal the ruling. It has also separately challenged the merger in a parallel process running in administrative court.

"We are disappointed in this outcome given the clear threat this merger poses to open competition in cloud gaming, subscription services, and consoles," FTC spokesperson Douglas Farrar said. "In the coming days we'll be announcing our next step to continue our fight to preserve competition and protect consumers."

A US judge has rejected a request from regulators to block Microsoft's proposed acquisition of major games publisher Activision Blizzard, boosting the chances of the deal going through. The merger, valued at $69 billion, would be the largest in gaming industry history. Shares in Activision surged over 10% following the news. The judge stated that the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) had failed to prove that the deal would harm competition or limit access to Activision's games. The decision in the US indicates a positive outcome for Microsoft's acquisition, which has already been approved by the European Union. While an appeal is ongoing in the UK, Microsoft has announced its intention to address concerns raised by the UK's Competition and Markets Authority regarding the cloud gaming market.
modify "The chances of Microsoft taking over major games publisher Activision Blizzard have been given a big boost after a US judge rejected a request from US regulators to block the deal.'

Microsoft's takeover bid of major games publisher Activision Blizzard receives a significant boost as a US judge dismisses the request from regulators to block the deal. This development increases the likelihood of the acquisition proceeding as planned.

translate the following to english:

Tesla（美：TSLA） 電動車在全球尤其內地熱賣，也是不少司機的「Dream Car」。 Tesla在微博宣布，周五（14日）會在內地推出兒童版電動越野車Cyberquad，網上流傳售價為11,990元人民幣。據介紹，Tesla Cyberquad專為8至12歲而設，續航時間約1.5小時，續航里程13公里，時速最高約每小時8公里。

Tesla's electric cars, especially in mainland China, are highly sought after and considered the "Dream Car" for many drivers. Tesla announced on Weibo that they will launch a children's version of their electric off-road vehicle, Cyberquad, in mainland China on Friday, the 14th. It is rumored to be priced at 11,990 Chinese yuan. The Tesla Cyberquad is designed for children aged 8 to 12, with a battery life of approximately 1.5 hours and a range of 13 kilometers. The top speed is around 8 kilometers per hour.

modify "Economists predict that the Reserve Bank of New Zealand will keep interest rates unchanged on Wednesday, marking the end of 12 consecutive rate hikes.The Bank of Canada will announce its interest rate decision on Wednesday, and all six major commercial banks in the country predict a 25 basis point rate hike. Forex strategists at institutions like Macquarie predict that the Canadian dollar will decline over 4% by the end of the year."

Economists foresee the Reserve Bank of New Zealand maintaining unchanged interest rates on Wednesday, signaling the conclusion of 12 consecutive rate hikes. Meanwhile, the Bank of Canada is set to announce its interest rate decision on the same day, with all six major commercial banks in the country predicting a 25 basis point increase. Analysts, including forex strategists from Macquarie, anticipate a depreciation of over 4% in the Canadian dollar by year-end.
